scope: Dynamic unique-group route-BMM floor for actual LightningIndexer compressed dKV.
read: Compacting actual selected compressed groups per query tile gives good route density, but doing torch.unique/scatter/BMM outside a fused kernel is overwhelmingly slow. This branch strengthens the requirement that unique-group compaction must happen cheaply inside the query-major D16 kernel.

interpretation.positive: Dynamic route compaction lowers S4096 layer-2 dense route slots to 1.344x the valid selected pairs, better than the best fixed-panel density points.
interpretation.negative: External PyTorch unique/scatter/BMM loops are orders of magnitude too slow: S4096 is 3.22 seconds and S128 is already 8.06 ms.
interpretation.next_step: Do not pursue external unique route-BMM. If using dynamic unique groups, implement compaction and reduction inside the query-major D16 sparse attention kernel, or fuse LI/compressor gradients to avoid this compressed dKV write path.
